item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Im currently reading the golden compass and that sent shivers down my spine. Also I need to learn more about this pataphysical department…

+1

A unsua

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

A pretty simple recommendation here. The last thing you want is this entity getting at your other anomalies of a similar nature lest one destroy the other, or something far, far worse happens. Recommendations include SCP-423, SCP-3143, and SCP-583. As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

SCP-3167 is currently at large; Pataphysics Task Force Alpha-4 ("Red Retcon Rangers") are assigned to tracking SCP-3167 through narrative space, with the goal of eventual containment or apprehension. SCP-3167's recent pattern of behavior indicates a special interest in the fantasy genre; as such, Foundation webcrawlers have been assigned to monitor discussions of contemporary fantasy and horror series for discussions of narrative deviations.

To this:

SCP-3167 is currently at large; Pataphysics Task Force Alpha-4 ("Red Retcon Rangers") are assigned to tracking SCP-3167 through narrative space, with the goal of eventual containment or apprehension. SCP-3167's recent pattern of behavior indicates a special interest in the fantasy genre; as such, Foundation webcrawlers have been assigned to monitor discussions of contemporary fantasy and horror series for discussions of narrative deviations. Additionally, prevention of any interaction between SCP-423, SCP-583, and SCP-3143 is to remain a high priority.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a fair interpretation, though not literally speaking. Certainly it is a great violation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the skip (and have upvoted) but;

Rowling wanted Weasley to die.

Pretty sure that's not true. And while the Doyle one is true I'm damn sure Tolkien didn't want Gandalf dead or he'd not have brought him back. I assume 3167 just thinks (or is trying to convince others that he thinks) he's doing the authors will.

Edit: A thought occurred to me; could (or would) he infect the same narrative more than once? As in create one version of the Harry Potter series where Ron dies and another where Harry dies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
handling one's dæmon without their consent is tantamount to rape

…Is that true? I have no idea…I thought it is like touching the private part

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I donno. I think I'm getting a little tired of pataphysics agents getting used this way. I just kinda feel like, with all the wild potential that radiated off of To Be Noir Not To Be, agent as invented interviewer who gets the tables turned on them by a metafictional entity is about the most boring.

And the death itself was clever, but I'm really not sure how it's supposed to tie into the SCP article and who the author is, and whether or not we're now affected? Am I missing some logic?

No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is metafiction (or patafiction? Pataphysics? Metaphysics? I don't know any more) done exceedingly well. It doesn't drag, it all comes together by the end, and while Holmes entry gave me enough information to partially figure out the ending for myself, it didn't detract from the narrative at all. Very good indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When Fred meets SCP-583.

Still, an entertaining read with a solid log.

+1 Ihp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really want to read the LOTR version. Still, I find this a bit of a strange skip. I don't know why I don't like it, this is the exact type of fanfic I love reading but something about this skip just doesn't sit well. I think it might be probably because it's that meta hero skip from series 3 but as a villain. Idk the number.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll probably go and clarify this once the contest is over (the 'no significant edits rule' applies), but it's meant to be a few copies that get fucked with, and people who read those copies have their perceptions altered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading about narratives getting wrecked was fun, and the ending was not what I expected at all.

+1 for more pataphysics goodness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, does Bob like permanently ruin Sherlock Holmes and Harry Potter and stuff for everyone, or just certain copies of these books?

Like in the Foundation universe does Lord of The Rings end like that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That final log is really goofy (is "fictional" supposed to be misspelled?) and this just comes off as an excuse to write fanfiction. >.>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Have you metafictional character lately?

This was oddly satisfying and satisfyingly odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had a big ol' grin while reading this. The anomaly is basically what I figured it would be when you mentioned the title in chat, but man, the execution. And that ending—yowza! Contests aside, this is one damn fine skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the type of stuff that is begging for a collab log.

item.title: Tonight on Coldpost Theater: A Story of a Serial Killer, written for the 72 Jam Hour Contest.
item.content:encoded:
I can take absolutely zero credit for the title pun— either of them. The idea of a serial killer that kills serial stories, as well as a Character Assassin, is taken from the wonderfully metafictional Thursday Next series by Jasper Fforde— well, wonderful until Book 7. Then it gets weird.

I came up with this, from concept to posting, in less than three hours, and it probably shows— there's not much 'mystery' in this 'murder mystery'. The last time I coldposted an SCP, it went over as well as a greased-up tire in San Francisco— it went downhill fast.

Hopefully you'll enjoy it. Happy contesting, y'all.
